Prerequisites & Server Setup

What you need before deploying clients
✓
Server Already Running — Starting Point

The Velociraptor server is already installed and running at https://192.168.1.16:8889. The server config is located at /etc/velociraptor/server.config.yaml.

At this point, no clients are enrolled yet — the client list is empty:

Velociraptor GUI showing 0 enrolled clients before deployment
RequirementDetail
Server OSUbuntu 24.04
Server IP192.168.1.16
GUI URLhttps://192.168.1.16:8889
Client port8000 (must be reachable from clients)
Velociraptor version0.76.1
Server config/etc/velociraptor/server.config.yaml
🐧

Part 1 — Ubuntu 24.04 Client

Client IP: 192.168.1.254 | Transfer method: SCP
1
Generate the Client Config (on the Server)

The client.config.yaml is extracted from the server config. It contains the server URL, CA certificate, and enrollment nonce — but no secrets. Run this on the Velociraptor server:

⚠️
Use sudo tee — not sudo cat > The redirect > runs as your user, not as root, so it will fail with "Permission denied" on /etc/velociraptor/. Always use | sudo tee to write privileged files.
sudo velociraptor --config /etc/velociraptor/server.config.yaml config client \
  | sudo tee /etc/velociraptor/client.config.yaml > /dev/null

sudo chmod 600 /etc/velociraptor/client.config.yaml
sudo chown root:root /etc/velociraptor/client.config.yaml

# Verify
ls -la /etc/velociraptor/

Expected output:

-rw------- 1 root root 2662 Apr 21 ... client.config.yaml
-rw-rw-r-- 1 cesar cesar 12666 Apr 20 ... server.config.yaml
2
Transfer the Client Config via SCP

Push the client config from the server to the Ubuntu client machine. Run this on the server:

sudo scp /etc/velociraptor/client.config.yaml cesar@192.168.1.254:/home/cesar/
ℹ️
On first connection, SSH will ask you to confirm the host fingerprint. Type yes and press Enter. You will then be prompted for the password of the user on the client machine.
SCP pushing client.config.yaml from server (192.168.1.16) to Ubuntu client (192.168.1.254)

On the Ubuntu client, verify the file arrived:

ls ~/
# Expected: client.config.yaml
client.config.yaml confirmed in the home directory of the Ubuntu client
3
Secure the Config File (on the Ubuntu Client)

Move the config to its permanent location and lock down permissions. Run on the Ubuntu client:

sudo mkdir -p /etc/velociraptor
sudo mv /home/cesar/client.config.yaml /etc/velociraptor/client.config.yaml
sudo chmod 600 /etc/velociraptor/client.config.yaml
sudo chown root:root /etc/velociraptor/client.config.yaml

# Verify
ls -la /etc/velociraptor/
Config secured at /etc/velociraptor/ with 600 permissions owned by root

Expected output:

-rw------- 1 root root 2662 Apr 21 04:38 client.config.yaml
CheckExpectedWhy
Permissions-rw------- (600)Only root can read the config
Ownerroot rootService runs as root
Size2662 bytesConfirms complete transfer
💡
Optional — Verify Config Contents

Confirm the config points to the correct server before proceeding:

sudo head -20 /etc/velociraptor/client.config.yaml

You should see server_urls pointing to https://192.168.1.16:8000/ and a valid ca_certificate block:

version:
  name: velociraptor
  version: 0.76.1
Client:
  server_urls:
  - https://192.168.1.16:8000/
  ca_certificate: |
    -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
    ...
4
Download the Velociraptor Binary

Download the Linux amd64 binary directly from GitHub to /usr/local/bin/:

sudo curl -L -o /usr/local/bin/velociraptor \
  "https://github.com/Velocidex/velociraptor/releases/download/v0.76/velociraptor-v0.76.1-linux-amd64"

sudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/velociraptor

# Verify
velociraptor version
Binary downloaded (80.9MB at 44.2MB/s) and version confirmed as 0.76.1

Expected version output:

name: velociraptor
version: 0.76.1
system: linux
architecture: amd64
5
Test the Connection

Before installing as a service, verify the client can reach the server and enroll successfully:

sudo velociraptor --config /etc/velociraptor/client.config.yaml client -v
Client connecting to https://192.168.1.16:8000/ — enrollment messages and 200 responses confirm success
✅
Signs of successful connection: You should see "Enrolling", "Received PEM for VelociraptorServer", "Connected to https://192.168.1.16:8000/reader", and HTTP status 200 responses. Press Ctrl+C once confirmed.

You can also verify enrollment in the GUI immediately — the client will appear with a green dot:

Ubuntu client (cesar / ubuntu24.04) appearing in the Velociraptor GUI with a green connected indicator
6
Install as a Persistent systemd Service

Create the systemd unit file using sudo tee (not sudo cat >) to write to the privileged directory:

sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/velociraptor_client.service << 'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=Velociraptor Endpoint Agent
Documentation=https://docs.velociraptor.app
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/velociraptor \
    --config /etc/velociraptor/client.config.yaml \
    client
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=10
StartLimitIntervalSec=60
StartLimitBurst=3
User=root
StandardOutput=journal
StandardError=journal
SyslogIdentifier=velociraptor-client

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF
systemd unit file being written with sudo tee — the file content is echoed to confirm it was written correctly

Now enable and start the service:

sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable velociraptor_client
sudo systemctl start velociraptor_client
sudo systemctl status velociraptor_client

Expected status output:

● velociraptor_client.service - Velociraptor Endpoint Agent
     Loaded: loaded (/etc/systemd/system/velociraptor_client.service; enabled)
     Active: active (running) since Tue 2026-04-21 04:44:15 UTC; 11ms ago
   Main PID: 1785 (velociraptor)
      Tasks: 6 (limit: 2262)
     Memory: 6.0M
        CPU: 9ms
ℹ️
Service design notes: network-online.target waits for an actual routable network (not just interface up). Restart=on-failure means a manual systemctl stop won't trigger restart. StartLimitBurst=3 prevents infinite crash loops on config errors.
SettingValuePurpose
After/Wantsnetwork-online.targetWait for actual network route before starting
Restarton-failureAuto-restart on crash, not on manual stop
RestartSec10sWait 10s between restart attempts
StartLimitBurst3Stop retrying after 3 consecutive failures
SyslogIdentifiervelociraptor-clientEasy log filtering with journalctl
7
Verify Enrollment in the GUI

Open the Velociraptor GUI at https://192.168.1.16:8889, click the Search Clients icon, and search for all. The Ubuntu client should appear with a green dot.

✅
Ubuntu client deployment complete! The service will automatically start on every reboot and reconnect to the server.
🪟

Part 2 — Windows 11 Client

Client IP: 192.168.1.203 | Transfer method: Python HTTP Server → Browser download
1
Transfer the Client Config via Python HTTP Server

The easiest way to get the config file to a Windows machine is to serve it temporarily over HTTP from the server. On the Velociraptor server, navigate to the config directory and start a Python HTTP server:

cd /etc/velociraptor
sudo python3 -m http.server 9999
Python HTTP server running on port 9999 — the GET request for client.config.yaml (200) confirms the Windows machine downloaded it

On the Windows 11 machine, open a browser and navigate to:

http://192.168.1.16:9999
Directory listing showing client.config.yaml and server.config.yaml — click client.config.yaml to download it
⚠️
Security note: The Python HTTP server also exposes server.config.yaml which contains secrets. Only run this temporarily on a trusted network and stop it immediately after downloading (Ctrl+C on the server).

Click client.config.yaml to download it. It will save to your Downloads or Documents folder. After downloading, stop the HTTP server on the server with Ctrl+C.

Windows Downloads folder showing both the MSI installer and client.config.yaml ready to use

You can also verify the files from an elevated PowerShell:

ls C:\Users\cesar\Documents\
PowerShell confirming client.config.yaml (2662 bytes) and the MSI installer are present in Documents
2
Download the MSI Installer

On the Windows 11 machine, open a browser and go to the Velociraptor GitHub releases page:

https://github.com/Velocidex/velociraptor/releases/tag/v0.76

Download velociraptor-v0.76.1-windows-amd64.msi (26 MB) — this is the correct installer for 64-bit Windows 11:

GitHub releases page — download velociraptor-v0.76.1-windows-amd64.msi (highlighted, 26MB) for Windows 11 64-bit
3
Run the MSI Installer

In File Explorer, navigate to your Downloads folder and double-click the MSI file:

velociraptor-v0.76.1-windows-amd64.msi in the Downloads folder — double-click to launch the installer

Windows UAC will prompt for administrator confirmation — click Yes:

User Account Control prompt — click Yes to allow the MSI to install Velociraptor
ℹ️
The MSI installs silently and quickly. It creates C:\Program Files\Velociraptor\, installs the binary and a placeholder config, and registers the Velociraptor Windows service automatically.

After installation, the Velociraptor folder will contain the binary and a placeholder client.config.yaml (24 KB — this is not your real config):

C:\Program Files\Velociraptor\ after MSI install — note the placeholder client.config.yaml (24 KB) which must be replaced
4
Replace the Client Config

The MSI installs a generic placeholder config. You must replace it with your server's client.config.yaml. Copy the file you downloaded in Step 1 and paste it into C:\Program Files\Velociraptor\:

Windows will ask if you want to replace the existing file — click "Replace the file in the destination":

Windows file copy dialog — click "Replace the file in the destination" to overwrite the placeholder with your real config

After replacing, the folder should show the updated client.config.yaml (3 KB — your real config) along with the writeback files that confirm the service connected:

C:\Program Files\Velociraptor\ after config replacement — velociraptor.writeback.yaml confirms the service started and enrolled successfully
✅
The writeback files are a good sign! velociraptor.writeback.yaml and velociraptor.writeback.yaml.bak are created automatically when the Velociraptor service successfully starts and connects to the server. Their presence confirms enrollment.
5
Verify Enrollment in the GUI

Open the Velociraptor GUI at https://192.168.1.16:8889 and search for all. Both clients should now appear with green dots:

Both clients enrolled and online — Windows 11 (DESKTOP-BNR2SCH) and Ubuntu 24.04 (cesar) both showing green connected status

🎉 Deployment Complete!

Both clients are enrolled, online, and managed by the Velociraptor server at 192.168.1.16

Client IDHostnameOSStatus
C.06b3ada7ff348885DESKTOP-BNR2SCHWindows 11 Pro 25H2🟢 Connected
C.4e0f18797b4a154fcesarUbuntu 24.04🟢 Connected
📚

Quick Reference

Commands and file locations for both platforms
📁
File Locations

🐧 Ubuntu 24.04

FilePath
Binary/usr/local/bin/velociraptor
Client config/etc/velociraptor/client.config.yaml
Service unit/etc/systemd/system/velociraptor_client.service
Writeback/etc/velociraptor.writeback.yaml

🪟 Windows 11

FilePath
BinaryC:\Program Files\Velociraptor\Velociraptor.exe
Client configC:\Program Files\Velociraptor\client.config.yaml
Service nameVelociraptor
WritebackC:\Program Files\Velociraptor\velociraptor.writeback.yaml
⚙️
Service Management Commands

🐧 Ubuntu — systemd

# Check status
sudo systemctl status velociraptor_client

# View live logs
journalctl -u velociraptor_client -f

# Last 50 log lines
journalctl -u velociraptor_client -n 50

# Restart service
sudo systemctl restart velociraptor_client

# Stop service
sudo systemctl stop velociraptor_client

# Remove service
sudo systemctl stop velociraptor_client
sudo systemctl disable velociraptor_client
sudo rm /etc/systemd/system/velociraptor_client.service
sudo systemctl daemon-reload

🪟 Windows — PowerShell (Admin)

# Check status
Get-Service -Name "Velociraptor"

# Restart service
Restart-Service -Name "Velociraptor"

# Stop service
Stop-Service -Name "Velociraptor"

# Start service
Start-Service -Name "Velociraptor"

# View logs (Event Viewer)
Get-EventLog -LogName System `
  -Source "Velociraptor" -Newest 20

# Remove service
Stop-Service -Name "Velociraptor"
sc.exe delete Velociraptor
🔄
Regenerating the Client Config (Server Side)

If the client config is ever lost or needs to be regenerated from the server config:

sudo velociraptor --config /etc/velociraptor/server.config.yaml config client \
  | sudo tee /etc/velociraptor/client.config.yaml > /dev/null

sudo chmod 600 /etc/velociraptor/client.config.yaml
sudo chown root:root /etc/velociraptor/client.config.yaml
ℹ️
The [ERROR] ClientInfo Manager: SaveSnapshot message that appears during config generation is harmless — it just means the server attempted to save a snapshot while running in client-config extraction mode.
🔧

Troubleshooting

Common issues and fixes
SymptomLikely CauseFix
"Permission denied" when writing filesUsing sudo cat > instead of sudo teeAlways use | sudo tee filename > /dev/null for privileged writes
Client not in GUI after 2–3 minService not running or config wrongCheck systemctl status velociraptor_client or Get-Service Velociraptor
Connection refused on port 8000Firewall blockingOpen port 8000 outbound on client to server
Certificate errors in logsWrong client config (using server config)Regenerate client.config.yaml from server
MSI "cannot be opened" errorRunning msiexec without full pathUse full path or double-click the file in Explorer
Windows client enrolls then disappearsPlaceholder config not replacedReplace C:\Program Files\Velociraptor\client.config.yaml with your real config
Clock skew errorsTime difference between client and serverSync time: timedatectl (Linux) / w32tm /resync (Windows)
Velociraptor v0.76.1 — Internal Deployment Guide
